title: Machine Learning/ Artificial Intelligence Engineer
company_focus:
Investigative analytics software solutions for crime
prevention and security
description:
Today’s world is crime-riddled. Criminals are everywhere,
invisible, virtual and sophisticated. Traditional ways to
prevent and investigate crime and terror are no longer
enough…

Technology is changing incredibly fast. The criminals know
it, and they are taking advantage. We know it too.

For nearly 30 years, the incredible minds at Cognyte around
the world have worked closely together and put their
expertise to work, to keep up with constantly evolving
technological and criminal trends, and help make the world a
safer place with leading investigative analytics software
solutions.

We are defined by our dedication to doing good and this
translates to business success, meaningful work friendships,
a can-do attitude, and deep curiosity.

As a Cognyter you will:

- We’re looking for an analyst to examine encrypted raw
  network data, extract TLS and IP-level metadata, and
  identify patterns or trends within complex data streams.
- You’ll build analysis pipelines and ensure the accuracy of
  detection methods using well-defined evaluation metrics.
- Candidates must demonstrate strong knowledge of network
  protocols, hands-on experience with packet capture
  analysis, and solid data analysis or machine learning
  skills.
- You will gain crypto domain expertise and be responsible for
  developing a Blockchain analytics product.

For that mission you’ll need:

- ﻿Bachelor’s degree – Computer Science / Information
  technology
- Having 2+ experience into Machine Learning/ Artificial
  Intelligence Engineer
- Nice to have prior experience in network traffic forensics
  or application traffic analysis,
- Deep understanding of network protocols (Ethernet, IP, TCP,
  UDP, DNS, etc.), would be plus
- Familiarity with TLS internals
- Nice to have working with network traffic data to extract
  meaningful characteristics or patterns from packet streams
- Knowlege Wireshark/tshark, Network Sniffers and protocol
  dissectors
- Experience with Python or any other language for scripting
  and automation would be plus
- Research-level machine learning expertise
- Fluent English
- Attention to detail and a high standard of data handling
- Highly committed and responsible, independent and able to
  take initiative
- Flexible and easy to adapt working on multiple technical
  contexts
- Able to work in a dynamic environment

Nice to have:

- AI models knowledge
- Experience creating labeled datasets and synthetic capture
  generation
- Experience with NoSQL databases (e.g., MongoDB)
- Experience with CI/CD solutions (e.g., Jenkins, TeamCity,
  Gitlab CI)
- Experience with Linux and Windows operating systems
- Understanding the SDLC – software Dev. lifecycle -> being
  part from designing to execution -> writing tests,
  executing, open bugs.
- Previous experience in a SCRUM team
responsibilities: Examine encrypted raw network data and extract TLS and IP-level metadata
Identify patterns or trends within complex data streams
Build analysis pipelines for network traffic analysis
Ensure accuracy of detection methods using well-defined evaluation metrics
Develop a Blockchain analytics product and gain crypto domain expertise
Work with packet capture analysis and network forensics
use_cases: Network traffic forensics and analysis for security investigations
Encrypted traffic classification and pattern detection
Automated detection of malicious network activity
Crime prevention and investigation through data analytics
ai_classification_reasoning:
This role focuses on applying machine learning to network
traffic analysis, specifically for extracting patterns from
encrypted data, TLS/IP metadata, and building detection
methods. While it mentions AI models and blockchain
analytics, the core work involves traditional ML techniques
for pattern recognition in network data rather than building
LLMs or GenAI systems.

domains: Network protocols (Ethernet, IP, TCP, UDP, DNS)
